ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No specific FoodEx2 code exists for butter rice cake. A0012 (Rice and rice products) is the closest defensible parent category.

serving.natural_language_portion: One serving of butter rice cake is typically a single piece weighing approximately 50 grams.

nutrient_source.title: Butter Rice Cake — adapted from HPB Singapore Foodo rice cake entry and USDA FoodData Central (Butter, unsalted; Whole milk)
nutrient_source.organization: HPB Singapore Foodo / USDA FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Core macros (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sugar, sodium, dietary fibre, potassium, calcium, magnesium, iron, cholesterol) are taken directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo record for this dish. Saturated fat, monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, vitamin A, and water are estimated from USDA FoodData Central entries for unsalted butter and whole milk, proportional to the described ingredient composition, and documented in notes.

gi_source.title: Mochi (rice cake) — Glycemic Index entry
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI estimated from mochi (rice cake) in the University of Sydney GI Database, a structurally similar glutinous rice product. The addition of butter and milk may marginally lower the effective GI due to fat-mediated gastric slowing; a medium GI category (65) is a conservative and defensible estimate. GL per serving calculated as (65 × 25.19 × 0.50) / 100 = 8.2.

hypertension.key_factor: Borderline sodium (231 mg/100g) partially offset by high potassium (327 mg/100g); saturated fat from butter adds secondary cardiovascular load.
hypertension.guidance: Limit portion to one piece and use unsalted butter to reduce sodium; the favourable potassium-to-sodium ratio supports DASH diet principles but total sodium from accompaniments must also be monitored.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium glycemic index (~65) with 25.2 g carbohydrate per 100 g; dietary fibre (3.7 g) and fat content moderate postprandial glucose rise.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Restrict to one piece per sitting, pair with a protein source to blunt glycaemic response, and avoid sweet toppings or sauces that would add additional sugar load.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (~2.5 g/100g) and trans fat (~0.1 g/100g) from butter contribute to atherogenic lipid load.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Consume in moderation; substituting butter with extra-virgin olive oil would reduce saturated and trans fat while introducing cardioprotective monounsaturated fatty acids.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Butter Rice Cake (黄油年糕, Kek Beras Mentega) is a Singaporean Chinese snack or dessert item with a crispy golden exterior and a chewy, springy interior. It is prepared from a batter of rice flour, whole milk, and butter that is baked and optionally pan-fried to develop the characteristic crisp shell. The interior remains soft, sticky, and mildly sweet, with a pronounced buttery and milky aroma. Per 100 g it provides 164 kcal, 6.5 g protein, 4.2 g fat, and 25.2 g carbohydrate, with a useful 3.7 g dietary fibre and notable potassium (327 mg) and iron (2.55 mg). The medium glycemic index (~65) means portion control is important for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes. Saturated fat from butter warrants moderation for those with cardiovascular disease or hypertension. The dish is gluten-free and vegetarian but contains dairy allergens.
common_user_queries: How is butter rice cake made?
Nutritional value of butter rice cake
Is butter rice cake gluten free?

confidence_reason: Core macros are taken directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo record for this dish (confidence 0.95). Saturated fat, monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, vitamin A, and water are estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data for butter and milk; GI is an analogous estimate from mochi. Overall confidence is therefore medium.
notes: Saturated_fat_g (2.5), monounsaturated_fat_g (1.1), polyunsaturated_fat_g (0.2), trans_fat_g (0.1), vitamin_a_mcg_rae (30.0), and water_g (60.0) are estimated from USDA FoodData Central entries for unsalted butter (FDC ID 173410) and whole milk (FDC ID 171265), proportional to the described ingredient composition, as detailed fatty acid and micronutrient breakdowns for this specific dish are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database. Vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, and selenium_mcg are set to -1: detailed micronutrient data for this specific butter rice cake preparation are not available in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026-04-17. Cholesterol reported as 0.0 in the HPB source record; revised to 10.0 mg based on estimated butter and milk contribution from USDA FoodData Central to avoid a confirmed-zero claim without explicit source support.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Normalized record to RESULT template; populated all mandatory fields.
Iteration 2: Added multilingual local names and aliases; added ontology codes; expanded LLM description; added health context for all three required conditions.

Iteration 3 (this iteration): Fixed g5 — changed foodex2_match_type from 'broad' to 'closest_parent' (valid enum value). Fixed q8 — audited all aliases_by_language string values against flat aliases array; added missing Chinese script forms (黄油年糕, 黃油年糕) to flat aliases; corrected hindi alias to exactly match flat alias (बटर राइस केक); removed Korean entry from aliases_by_language as dish is not Korean; replaced 'Butter Tteok' with 'Butter Nian Gao' as the more culturally appropriate English alias; verified full consistency between aliases_by_language and flat aliases.
